rationale: Falsifiability mechanics all check out at e6dfa4cf: TestBranchExistsE_UnreadableRefStore grep exits 1; BranchExistsE, ScanOrphanedClosedBeads, TestBranchExistsE_ThreeWay, and any show-ref probe are 0 Go hits at e6dfa4cf and 4cbf437b; AC1 pairs the new test with an existence discriminator and an exact-named -run; the Validation-Proofs exact-named-run claim holds for all 16 named tests. But the fix's load-bearing empirical claim is FALSE. Reproduced in a /tmp throwaway repo (git 2.51.2, files backend): git show-ref --verify refs/heads/<absent> WITHOUT --quiet exits 128 (fatal: not a valid ref), not 1 — identical exit code AND identical stderr to the existing-branch-with-chmod-000-refs/heads case (also 128). --quiet is precisely what switches show-ref from die()/128 to the scripted exit-1 path. So the specified three-way has no exit-1 leg: TestBranchExistsE_ThreeWay's absent->(false,nil) assertion is unimplementable via the mandated probe, an honoring implementation refuses on every merged-and-cleaned deleted branch (the round-3 false-refusal regression, AC2(d) red), and exit code alone still cannot separate genuine absence from an unreadable store — the round-4 masking finding is not actually closed.
concrete_changes_required: Correct the probe evidence in R1, Scope internal/gitutil, and OQ2 to the actual git 2.51.2 behavior: show-ref --verify WITHOUT --quiet exits 128 for BOTH genuine absence and an unreadable loose-ref store, with identical 'not a valid ref' stderr; withdraw the 'exit 1 -> genuinely absent' leg and the 'only probe whose exit code alone gives a clean three-way' claim (the round-4 table's absent=1 row does not reproduce).

Re-specify BranchExistsE on a reproducible contract using a second signal, since no single git exit code separates absent from unreadable in the files backend: e.g. show-ref --verify --quiet exit-1 as candidate-absence CONFIRMED by an explicit ref-store-health probe (loose refs/heads enumerable + packed-refs readable, or a successful full show-ref/for-each-ref enumeration); any health-probe failure -> (false, non-nil error) -> gate refuses. Keep the general fail-closed-on-ambiguity principle.

Update AC1(c)'s TestBranchExistsE_ThreeWay and TestBranchExistsE_UnreadableRefStore assertions to the corrected contract so both tests are simultaneously satisfiable by one implementation and AC2(d) TestApproveImpl_DeletedBranchNoRefusal stays green, and re-verify every exit-code citation in the spec empirically at the new SHA.

findings.finding: Round-4 fix grade: the fix text landed exactly as consolidated (probe re-specified, general principle stated, defense-in-depth note added, new test named with discriminator + exact-named run — mechanically ADDRESSED), but its empirical foundation is wrong, which makes it functionally MISSED on the discrimination goal and introduces a new defect. Verified on git 2.51.2 (files backend): git show-ref --verify refs/heads/<name> without --quiet returns 0 (present), 128 (absent, 'fatal: not a valid ref'), 128 (existing branch + chmod 000 .git/refs/heads, same message). The spec's 0/1/128 three-way does not exist; absent and unreadable are indistinguishable by exit code and stderr. Under the specified contract every genuinely-deleted (normal post-complete) branch yields a non-nil error and the gate refuses — violating R1's own falsifier ('genuinely-absent branch yields anything but (false,nil)') and AC2(d) — while the unreadable-store masking the round-4 finding targeted remains unclosed by this primitive. All falsifiability discriminators otherwise RED as claimed at e6dfa4cf and 4cbf437b.
